message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first TUI query + mute paths (tick 255)

Migrates 5 ambient storage calls across 2 TUI handler methods in
`frankenterm-core/tui/query.rs` to their `_with_cx` siblings.

**`list_events` (L488 block, shared `query_cx`):**

- `storage.get_events(query)` → `get_events_with_cx(&query_cx, query)`
- `storage.get_event_annotations(event.id)` →
  `get_event_annotations_with_cx(&query_cx, event.id)` (loop-shared cx
  declared above the `for`, avoids re-resolving per iteration — same
  pattern as tick 249 L26201)

**`mark_event_muted` (L794 block, shared `mute_cx`):**

- `storage.get_event_identity_key(event_id)` →
  `get_event_identity_key_with_cx(&mute_cx, event_id)`
- `storage.add_event_mute(record)` → `add_event_mute_with_cx(&mute_cx, record)`
- `storage.mark_event_handled(event_id, None, "muted")` →
  `mark_event_handled_with_cx(&mute_cx, event_id, None, "muted")`

Both handler methods are sync fns that wrap an inner `runtime.block_on`
closure. The cx bindings are resolved inside the async closure via
`Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)` — TUI handlers don't thread
Cx through their sync call graph so call-time resolution is the only
option. This picks up whatever Cx the TUI runtime has active, enabling
a cx-cancel during TUI operation to propagate through the event query
and mute-write pipeline.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 69 (187-255).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 14 (runtime.rs 7 + mcp.rs 2 + tui/query.rs 5).
main.rs cross-crate total: 127 sites (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
